name: ai-coding-skills
description: Portable Claude Code + Codex CLI skills: the orchestrate dual-brain shipping loop and the pipeline coverage-matrix workflow.

plugins.name: orchestrate
plugins.description: Dual-brain plan → execute → review → ship loop: Claude plans and reviews the PR, OpenAI Codex CLI critiques the plan, writes the code, opens the PR, and applies review edits. Deploy is risk-gated. Skill only — run orchestrate/install.sh from a clone for the dashboard, driver, and Codex-side skill.

plugins.name: pipeline
plugins.description: Standard delivery pipeline for non-trivial work: coverage matrix (security/risk/review/tests/docs), adaptive routing, verification gates, and PR-ready output. Routes real implementation into the orchestrate loop.

plugins.name: debug
plugins.description: Evidence-driven debugging: a cheap systematic single-pass loop that escalates hard bugs to a dual-brain council — Claude and Codex CLI research independently, hypotheses are falsified, and candidate fixes race in disposable worktrees. Token-conscious with a mandatory usage report.

plugins.name: critique
plugins.description: Fair-but-rigorous critical-friend pass on an idea, plan, prompt, or architecture before you commit — steelman, challenge, calibrated verdict. Modes: rubber-duck / critique / hard-challenge / decision. Can fan out parallel subagent lenses and pull an independent second-model opinion.

plugins.name: chief-of-staff
plugins.description: Turn one chat into the session's chief of staff: decompose a broad goal into parallel workstreams, delegate to background subagents, re-command workers mid-flight, route implementation to the orchestrate loop, and synthesize one answer. Intra-session by design.
